name: Type 3 Chi-Nu
summary: Japan's best mass-produced tank, capable of engaging the M4 Sherman on more equal terms. Held in reserve for the anticipated Allied invasion of the Home Islands that never came.

name: Type 94 Tankette
summary: Used extensively in China. Provided infantry support and logistic towing but was poorly armed and vulnerable to almost any anti-armour weapon.

name: Type 95 Ha-Go
summary: Japan's main light tank. Thin armour made it vulnerable to .50 calibre machine guns at close range. Used throughout the Pacific on jungle and island campaigns.

name: Type 97 Chi-Ha
summary: Japan's most-produced tank of the war. Effective against Chinese infantry in 1937; completely outclassed by Soviet armour at Khalkhyn Gol (1939) and by US M4 Shermans in the Pacific.
